content.text:
Great question. The next steps depend on what you want to achieve. Here are some common options and concrete next steps you can take:

- Explore data
  - Pick a database (admin, config, or local) and list its collections.
  - In the Mongo shell or a driver:
    - Use the database: use admin (or use config/local)
    - List collections: show collections (shell) or db.getCollectionNames()
    - Peek at data: db.<collection>.findOne() or db.<collection>.find().limit(5).toArray()

- Inspect security and users
  - In the admin database, list users and roles:
    - In the shell: use admin; db.getUsers(); db.getUser("<username>")

- Check health and configuration
  - Get server status and replica set info:
    - In the shell: db.serverStatus(); rs.status() (if part of a replica set)

- Plan backups or maintenance
  - Confirm backup schedules, oplog size, and storage configuration.

If you tell me which database you want to start with (admin, config, or local) and what you want to learn (structure, data, users, health), I can provide exact commands or guide you through the steps. I can also help draft small scripts or queries in your preferred driver (Node.js, Python, etc.).

tools.description: List databases in the connected MongoDB instance.
tools.name: list_databases
